description:
Agents routinely try 'bl ready --limit 20' (carrying the habit over from other tools) and get a parser error. Today 'bl ready' accepts only --json and --no-fetch, so when the ready queue is long they either dump the whole thing or try to hand-pipe the JSON through jq.

Goal: add an optional --limit N (perhaps also -n N) that truncates the ready queue to N entries, for both text and --json output. When the queue is longer than N, surface that fact:
- Text: footer line like '... and 12 more. Use --limit to see more.' (or similar — pick wording that doesn't imply the 12 are hidden due to a bug).
- JSON: still emit just the N entries; consider emitting a sibling 'truncated: true' / 'total: N' field, but keep backward compat with callers that expect the current top-level array. Simplest path: keep stdout as the truncated array and document the cap in the flag help.

Not in scope:
- Server-side pagination, cursors, or offset. --limit is a display cap, not a cursor.
- Changing the ready-queue ordering (priority + creation time today).

Acceptance:
- 'bl ready --limit 5' shows at most 5 entries.
- 'bl ready --limit 5 --json' returns an array of length <= 5.
- --limit 0 and negative values are rejected with a clear error (or --limit 0 means 'no cap', TBD but document it).
- A test covers truncation in both text and JSON modes.
title: bl ready --limit N to cap the ready queue
